title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your school's board of governors has asked you to write a report on the current use of technology in teaching and learning at your school. Your report should describe how technology is currently being used, highlight its benefits, identify any problems, and make recommendations for the future.
tasks.modelAnswer:
THE USE OF TECHNOLOGY IN TEACHING AND LEARNING AT CRANFORD SCHOOL

Introduction
This report evaluates the current deployment of technology across Cranford School, considers its effectiveness, identifies challenges, and offers recommendations to enhance its impact on teaching and learning.

Current Use
All classrooms are equipped with interactive whiteboards, and students in Years 10 to 13 are issued personal laptops. A virtual learning environment hosts lesson materials, assignments, and formative assessments. Several departments, notably Mathematics and Modern Languages, make active use of adaptive learning software.

Benefits
Teacher feedback indicates that technology has improved lesson pacing and enabled more differentiated instruction. Students report that access to recorded lessons has been particularly valuable for revision. The virtual learning environment has also strengthened communication between staff and parents regarding student progress.

Identified Problems
A significant minority of students in lower year groups lack reliable home internet access, limiting their ability to complete digital homework tasks. Additionally, inconsistent staff training means that some teachers use available technology minimally, creating an uneven experience for pupils across subjects.

Recommendations
The school should establish a device loan scheme to ensure all students can access online resources at home. A structured programme of continuing professional development in educational technology should be made compulsory for all teaching staff. Finally, a designated EdTech lead should be appointed to coordinate resources and share best practice across departments.

Conclusion
With targeted investment and staff development, Cranford School can realise the full potential of its existing technology infrastructure.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement for an online course that uses artificial intelligence to personalise language learning. You are interested in enrolling but have some questions. Write a letter to the course provider asking about the effectiveness of the AI system, what support is available from human teachers, the cost and duration of the course, and any free trial options.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Course Advisor,

I am writing to enquire about your AI-powered language learning course, which I came across in an advertisement in last month's edition of 'Language Today'. I am a B2-level Spanish learner hoping to reach C1 proficiency within the next twelve months, and your course appears to offer an approach that might suit my learning style.

I would be grateful if you could clarify a few points before I commit to enrolling. Firstly, I am keen to understand how the AI personalisation system functions in practice. Specifically, does the platform adapt in real time to errors and learning patterns, or does personalisation occur between sessions based on performance data? I am also curious whether the system has been independently evaluated for effectiveness at upper-intermediate and advanced levels.

Secondly, could you outline what access students have to human teachers? I am wary of courses that rely exclusively on automated feedback, as I believe nuanced guidance from an experienced tutor is irreplaceable, particularly for written production and pronunciation.

I would also appreciate details regarding the cost structure. Is the course priced as a one-off payment or a recurring subscription? Is there a significant difference in cost between monthly and annual plans?

Finally, I would welcome information about any free trial period, as I would like to assess the platform's interface and content before making a financial commitment.

I look forward to your response.

Yours faithfully,
Martin Berger

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: An educational technology magazine is asking readers to submit reviews of apps, websites, or software designed to support learning. Write a review describing what the tool does, assessing its strengths and weaknesses, and recommending whether it is worth using.
tasks.modelAnswer:
SMART REVISION, REAL RESULTS: A REVIEW OF ANKI

Anki is a free, open-source flashcard application that uses a spaced repetition algorithm to optimise long-term memory retention. Available on desktop and mobile platforms, it is widely used by language learners, medical students, and anyone seeking to memorise large volumes of information efficiently.

The application's core strength lies in its scientific foundation. Rather than presenting cards at fixed intervals, Anki adjusts the frequency of review based on how confidently the user recalls each item. Cards answered with ease are shown less frequently; those answered hesitantly or incorrectly appear more often. This approach, grounded in cognitive science research, makes study time considerably more efficient than passive re-reading.

Anki's flexibility is another significant asset. Users can create their own card decks for virtually any subject, incorporating text, images, audio, and even video. A vast library of pre-made decks is freely available through the platform's shared database, covering everything from Japanese vocabulary to anatomy.

The primary drawback is the learning curve associated with the software's interface, which is functional rather than elegant and can feel unintuitive for new users. Creating well-designed card decks also requires an upfront time investment that some learners may find discouraging.

Despite these limitations, Anki is an outstanding learning tool for disciplined, self-directed students. Those willing to invest time in setting it up will find it one of the most effective revision resources available — particularly for vocabulary acquisition in language learning contexts. Recommended without reservation.
